sections.title: Overview
sections.content: IV giving sets connect fluids or infusions to vascular access devices. In prehospital care, correct setup matters because air, mislabelling, uncontrolled flow, or wrong-line use can all cause avoidable harm.

sections.title: Types of Giving Sets
sections.content:
- Use the giving set appropriate to the fluid, blood product, medication, and required flow control
- Check local drop factors and device-specific instructions rather than relying on memory alone
- Label or identify special-purpose lines clearly when multiple infusions are in use

sections.title: Priming & Setup
sections.content:
1. Confirm the right fluid or infusion, bag integrity, expiry, and intended route

2. Prepare the giving set aseptically and close the flow control before spiking the bag

3. Prime the chamber and tubing fully, ensuring air is cleared before connection

4. Connect to the vascular access device and set the flow according to the clinical goal or prescription

5. Reassess the line regularly for infiltration, air, disconnection, and unintended flow changes

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- Air, misconnected lines, and wrong-fluid administration are high-risk but avoidable errors
- Blood product delivery needs the correct set and compatible fluids according to protocol
- Gravity rate changes with bag height, patient position, and cannula function, so visual rechecks matter
- Document what was connected, how it was run, and any line issues encountered
